Calibration Utilities

Source: R/model_utils.R
calibration_tools.Rd

These helpers support likelihood evaluation, reparameterisation by reproduction numbers, likelihood surfaces, and profile-likelihood summaries.

Usage

evaluate_negative_loglikelihood(
  beta_h,
  beta_m,
  observation_times,
  observed_cases,
  initial_state,
  time_grid,
  fixed_parameters
)

compute_reproduction_components(initial_state, fixed_parameters)

beta_m_from_reproduction(
  beta_h,
  reproduction_value,
  mode,
  initial_state,
  fixed_parameters
)

evaluate_reparameterised_loglikelihood(
  beta_h,
  reproduction_value,
  mode,
  observation_times,
  observed_cases,
  initial_state,
  time_grid,
  fixed_parameters
)

evaluate_loglikelihood_grid(x_values, y_values, evaluator)

relative_likelihood(loglikelihood)

profile_interval(values, relative_profile, cutoff = 0.146)

Arguments

beta_h

Human-to-mosquito transmission coefficient.

beta_m

Mosquito-to-human transmission coefficient.

observation_times

Observation times on the simulation grid.

observed_cases

Two-column matrix of observed classical and hemorrhagic case counts.

initial_state

Named numeric state vector used by run_model().

time_grid

Numeric vector of solver times.

fixed_parameters

Named numeric vector containing the fixed model parameters.

reproduction_value

Value of R01, R02, or R0, depending on mode.

mode

Character scalar selecting "R01", "R02", or "R0".

x_values

Numeric vector for the first parameter axis.

y_values

Numeric vector for the second parameter axis.

evaluator

Function taking a two-element numeric vector and returning a negative log-likelihood.

loglikelihood

Numeric vector or matrix of log-likelihood values.

values

Numeric vector defining the profile grid.

relative_profile

Numeric vector of relative profile likelihood values.

cutoff

Relative-likelihood cutoff used to define the interval.

Value

A scalar negative Poisson log-likelihood.

A named numeric vector with the reproduction-number components C1, C2, and C3.

The implied mosquito-to-human transmission coefficient betaM.

A scalar negative Poisson log-likelihood after converting the reproduction-number parameterisation back to betaM.

A matrix of log-likelihood values on the supplied grid.

Relative likelihood values normalised by the maximum entry.

A named numeric vector with entries lower and upper.

On this page

Developed by Manuel Cota.
